is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of uncomplicated falciparum malaria; analysed Pfk13 and Pfmdr1 in P. falciparum isolates.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Pfmdr1 codon N86Y analysed (target marker Pfmdr1:86). Also Pfk13 propeller codons 440-600 including target codons 446,458,476,493,539,543,553,561,568,574,580 etc.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 4 gives per-site N86 prevalence: Kibaha 79 (98.8), Mkuzi 87 (98.9), Mlimba 87 (98.9), Ujiji 88 (100.0), Total 341 (99.1); also 184F and D1246 counts.

sub_country_location.evidence: Frequencies broken down by four sentinel sites: Kibaha (Coast), Mkuzi (Tanga/Muheza), Mlimba (Morogoro/Kilombero), Ujiji (Kigoma), with health centre locations named.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: All samples collected at enrolment (day 0) between April and October 2016 - a single year window.

african_field_samples.evidence: Clinical isolates (DBS) collected from patients at four sites in mainland Tanzania.

is_primary_study.evidence: Single-arm prospective in vivo therapeutic efficacy study with primary molecular analysis.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples processed at CDC under 'US President's Malaria Initiative-supported Antimalarial Resistance Monitoring in Africa (PARMA) Network' and data shared with WHO as part of global TES monitoring; part of NMCP sentinel site surveillance. Potential overlap with network datasets.

supplement.evidence: Per-locus Pfmdr1 SNP frequencies by site are in Table 4 within the main text; Pfk13 non-synonymous mutations described in text. Supplemental Table 1 concerns adverse event timing, not marker frequencies.

assessment.summary: Therapeutic efficacy study of AL in mainland Tanzania (2016) at four sentinel sites reporting site-level Pfmdr1 SNP prevalences (including target codon 86) and Pfk13 propeller mutations. Provides extractable per-site frequencies within a single-year collection window, meeting all eligibility criteria. Some duplicate risk due to PARMA network/WHO TES data sharing.
